originalTitle: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
title: Caring for Our Friends in Nigeria
content:
Hello, amazing explorers and wonderful friends! Your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share some important news from a beautiful country called Nigeria. We always want to make sure everyone is safe and happy, especially our friends who live far away. Let's talk about something that happened, and how grown-ups are working to keep everyone well.

A Special Place Called a Market

Imagine a very busy and colourful place! That's a market. In a village in Nigeria, there was a market where people go to buy yummy fruits, fresh vegetables, beautiful clothes, and lots of other cool things. It's a place where friends meet, families laugh, and everyone feels happy. Markets are like big outdoor parties where you can find almost anything you need! Sometimes, grown-ups called 'militants' – these are people who sometimes cause trouble and are not very kind – can make places feel unsafe. Everyone wants to stop these people from causing problems.

What Happened in the Village?

Not long ago, there were reports that something happened near one of these happy markets in a village in Nigeria. We heard that big airplanes were flying in the sky. These airplanes are often trying to stop the people who cause trouble. But sometimes, when things are very fast and busy, mistakes can happen. We heard that maybe, just maybe, something went wrong, and the market was affected. We are worried that some people might have gotten hurt. Our biggest hope is that everyone is safe and sound now.

Who Helps Keep Us Safe?

In Nigeria, just like in many countries, there are brave men and women who work very hard to keep everyone safe. Some of these grown-ups fly big airplanes! They are part of something called the 'Nigerian Air Force'. Think of them like super-pilots who watch over the country. Their job is to protect people from those who want to cause trouble. When something big like this happens, everyone wants to know exactly what took place. The people in charge are still looking into it. It's like when you're trying to find a lost toy – sometimes it takes a little time to find out what happened, and they want to be very sure before they tell everyone.

Did You Know? Amazing Nigeria!

Nigeria is an amazing country in West Africa! It's super special because it has so many different people, speaking lots of different languages, and enjoying many different cultures. It's often called the 'Giant of Africa' because it has more people living there than any other country on our continent. Imagine how many stories and songs are shared there!

Did You Know? Markets Are Fun!

Markets are not just for shopping; they are also places where communities come alive! You can hear music, smell delicious foods cooking, and see bright colours everywhere. They are very important for villages and cities because people can buy what they need for their homes and sell things they have made or grown. It's a busy, happy hub!

Did You Know? Airplanes Have Many Jobs!

Some airplanes take us on holidays to see new places, some carry important packages like medicines or toys across the world, and some, like the ones used by the Air Force, help keep countries safe. They fly high above us, watching over everything and helping to protect people on the ground. They are like giant birds in the sky!

Why is Peace So Important?

Nobody likes to be worried or scared. We all want to live in peace. Peace means everyone is kind to each other, there are no fights, and everyone feels safe and loved in their homes and communities. When sad things like this happen, it reminds us how very important it is to be kind to everyone and to wish for peace for all people, everywhere in the world. If anyone gets hurt, doctors and nurses are the superheroes who help them feel better. They give medicines and kind care to make sure everyone heals quickly. We are thankful for all the helpers in the world!

What Can We Do?

Even though we are small, we can do big things! We can be kind to our friends at school, help our families at home, and learn a lot in our classes. When we grow up, we can help make the world an even better and safer place for everyone to live in. We can also think happy thoughts and send our love to all the people in Nigeria, hoping they are safe and healthy.

A Message of Hope and Safety

We know that grown-ups are working very, very hard to keep everyone in Nigeria safe. They are trying their best to understand what happened and make sure it doesn't happen again. Let's send our love and good wishes to all our friends in Nigeria. We hope for peace, lots of smiles, and safety for everyone! Remember, kindness and care can make a big difference in the world.
summary: We heard about something sad happening near a market in Nigeria, and grown-ups are working hard to keep everyone safe and find out exactly what happened.
whyMatters: It's important because everyone deserves to be safe and happy, especially in busy places like markets, and we want peace for all our friends in Nigeria and everywhere else.

originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Djibouti's Leader, Mr. Guelleh, Stays On as President!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter from Africa here, ready to share some big news in simple words. Today, we're zooming into a cool country called Djibouti! Can you say Dj-i-bou-ti? It's a special place on our amazing continent, Africa. We have some news about their leader, President Guelleh. He will keep being the president for another special time. Let's learn all about it!

Meet Djibouti! A Special African Country

First, let's find Djibouti on a map! It's in the eastern part of Africa, right next to the big blue ocean. It's a sunny place with beautiful beaches and even some volcanoes! People there speak French and Arabic, and they have yummy food. It's not a very big country, but it's full of interesting things. Imagine living by the sea every day! Did you know? Djibouti is famous for its Salt Lake, called Lake Assal. It's one of the saltiest lakes in the world, even saltier than the ocean! It looks like a giant white mirror. It's a truly amazing natural wonder!

Meet Mr. Guelleh, Djibouti's President

Every country needs a leader, just like your school has a principal or your team has a captain. In Djibouti, the leader is called a President. His name is Mr. Ismail Omar Guelleh. He is 78 years old. That's older than most of your grandparents! He has been the president for a very, very long time. Imagine being the captain of a ship for a long, long journey. Mr. Guelleh has been leading Djibouti for 27 years! That's longer than you've been alive, and probably longer than your parents have been alive too! It's a really, really long time to be in charge of a whole country.

What Does a President Do?

A President is like the main helper and decision-maker for the country. They work to make sure everyone has schools, hospitals, and good roads. They also help keep the country safe and peaceful. It's a very big job with lots of responsibilities. They have to think about what is best for all the people living in their country. Did you know? There are 54 countries in Africa, and almost all of them have a President as their leader!

How Leaders Are Chosen: A Vote!

When it's time to choose a leader, people in the country usually get to vote. Voting is like choosing your favorite color or your favorite game. Grown-ups go to special places and put a mark next to the name of the person they want to be the leader. The person with the most votes wins! It's how people show who they want to lead their country. In Djibouti, the people who voted chose Mr. Guelleh. Almost all of them, nearly 98 out of every 100 people who voted, picked him! That's a huge number of votes! This means many people wanted him to continue as their president.

Mr. Guelleh Stays On For His Sixth Time!

So, Mr. Guelleh has been the president for 27 years. And now, he will be president for even longer! This is his sixth time winning to be the leader. It's like winning the same race six times in a row! That's very special and very rare. Imagine playing a game, and you are the team captain, and everyone keeps choosing you to be the captain game after game after game. That's a bit like what has happened with Mr. Guelleh and the people of Djibouti.

Why Some People Didn't Take Part

Now, here's something important to know. In Djibouti, some other people who also wanted to be president decided not to join in the voting this time. They are called 'opposition candidates.' Sometimes, people decide not to take part in something because they feel it's not fair, or they have different ideas about how things should be done. So, even though Mr. Guelleh got almost all the votes, it's good to remember that not everyone had a chance to choose from many different people because some decided not to be in the election. It's like having a race where some of the fastest runners decide not to run.

What Happens Next in Djibouti?

So, Mr. Guelleh will continue to lead Djibouti. He will keep making plans and decisions for the country. His job will be to work for all the people, to help make their lives better, and to keep Djibouti a peaceful and happy place. Did you know? Djibouti is an important place for ships because it's right on a busy sea route! Many ships from all over the world pass by Djibouti every day carrying goods. It's like a big crossroads for the ocean!

Learning About Our World!

It's really cool to learn about different countries and their leaders, isn't it? Even though Djibouti is far away for some of us, we are all part of the big African family and the big world family. Learning about news helps us understand how different places work and what is happening around us. Thank you for joining me, brave news explorers! Keep asking questions and keep learning. The world is full of amazing stories!
summary: Mr. Guelleh, who has been the leader of Djibouti for a very long time, has won to be president again, and he will continue to guide his country.
whyMatters: This news is important because it tells us that the leader of a special African country, Djibouti, will stay the same, and he will keep working to make good things happen for everyone there.

originalTitle: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
title: A Star's Voice: Remembering Asha Bhosle's Happy Songs!
content:
Hello, hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! Your news reporter friend is here today with a special story from a far-off land called India. Have you ever heard a song that makes your feet want to dance all by themselves? Or a tune that makes you feel super happy inside? Well, today we are talking about a very special lady who made millions of people feel exactly like that!

Who Was Asha Bhosle?

Her name was Asha Bhosle. She was a famous, famous singer from India. Imagine someone who sings almost every day for many, many years! That was Asha Bhosle. She had a voice that was so special, it was like a beautiful rainbow of sounds. It made people smile, it made them laugh, and it sometimes even made them want to cry happy tears. She lived for a very, very long time, until she was 92 years old. That's older than most grandmas and grandpas!

What is Bollywood?

Now, you might be asking, "What kind of songs did she sing?" Asha Bhosle sang mostly for something called Bollywood. Bollywood is a special name for movies made in India. These movies are not just talking; they are full of bright colours, amazing dancing, and lots and lots of songs! It's like a big, colourful party on the screen, and the songs are the heart of the party. Did you know? Bollywood makes more movies than Hollywood sometimes! They love singing and dancing in their stories.

A Voice Full of Sunshine

Asha Bhosle's voice was like sunshine. It could be playful and bouncy for happy songs, or soft and sweet for gentle songs. She could sing high notes and low notes, fast songs and slow songs. It was truly magical! When you heard her sing, you just had to stop and listen. Her voice reached into people's hearts and made them feel alive. It made the movies even more exciting and fun. She sang so many different kinds of songs. Some were for young, playful girls in the movies, and some were for wise, grown-up ladies. She could change her voice to fit any person in the story. It was like she had a hundred different voices all inside her!

Singing for the Stars

In Bollywood movies, the actors on screen often don't sing the songs themselves. Instead, a super talented singer like Asha Bhosle sings the song behind the scenes. Then, in the movie, it looks like the actor is singing! Asha Bhosle lent her voice to so many famous actresses over many years. She was like the secret singing star for many movie stars! Imagine if you were watching a movie and your favourite character started singing with a voice that filled the whole room. That was the magic Asha Bhosle created for millions. Her voice helped tell the story, showed what the characters were feeling, and made everyone want to hum along.

So Many Songs!

How many songs do you think Asha Bhosle sang? Take a guess! Did you guess a hundred? A thousand? Try many, many thousands! It's almost unbelievable. She sang more songs than most people could count in their whole lives. She even holds a world record for singing the most songs! Imagine practicing and singing so many different tunes. Did you know? She sang in over twenty different languages! That's like being able to talk to people from many different countries through your songs.

Her Music Reached Far and Wide

Even though Asha Bhosle was from India, her beautiful voice travelled all over the world. People in many countries, including here in Africa, loved to listen to her songs. Her music brought joy to people of all ages and all places. It showed how music can connect us all, no matter where we live or what language we speak. Her songs became the 'soundtrack' for many, many people's lives. A soundtrack is like the special music that plays during important parts of a story. For generations of Indians, her voice was the soundtrack to their happy moments, their sad moments, and their dancing moments.

A Special Family of Singers

Asha Bhosle came from a very musical family. Her older sister, Lata Mangeshkar, was also a super famous singer! Imagine having a sister who is also a singing superstar. They both shared their amazing voices with the world for many, many years. Music was a big part of their family life. Did you know? Asha Bhosle started singing when she was just a little girl, like many of you! She loved music from a very young age.

Saying Goodbye, But Her Music Stays

Now, Asha Bhosle lived a very long and full life, filled with music and joy. She recently said goodbye to the world, but her beautiful voice and all her wonderful songs will stay with us forever. When a very special person leaves us, we feel a little sad, but we can also remember all the happy times and the amazing things they did. Her voice will always be there, ready for us to listen, dance, and sing along to. It's a special gift she left for everyone. So, next time you hear a happy tune, think of Asha Bhosle and her sunny voice! She truly was a star whose music made the world a brighter, happier place. We thank Asha Bhosle for all the joy and music she brought to our world. Her songs will keep making people smile for many, many years to come!
summary: Asha Bhosle, a very famous singer from India, who sang thousands of joyful songs for Bollywood movies, recently passed away at 92, but her magical voice and music will live on forever.
whyMatters: Asha Bhosle's music brought happiness to millions of people for many years. Her songs made people dance and smile, and she showed everyone the amazing power of music to connect hearts across the world.

originalTitle: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
title: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
content: The Nigerian Air Force has not addressed reports it struck a village market while targeting militants.

originalTitle: Man City make statement in dominant win over Chelsea
title: Man City make statement in dominant win over Chelsea
content: Manchester City put three goals past Chelsea in a 17-minute spell at Stamford Bridge, to close the gap on leaders Arsenal to six points with a game in hand in the Premier League.

originalTitle: Sunderland sink Spurs in De Zerbi's opening game in charge
title: Sunderland sink Spurs in De Zerbi's opening game in charge
content: Sunderland's Nordi Mukiele scores the only goal in the Black Cats 1-0 home win against Tottenham, handing Roberto De Zerbi a loss in his first game in charge as Tottenham manager. The result leaves Spurs in the Premier League relegation zone.

originalTitle: Forest hit back to earn crucial point against Villa
title: Forest hit back to earn crucial point against Villa
content: Neco Williams scores his first goal since September to earn a 1-1 draw for Nottingham Forest against Aston Villa at the City Ground.
